title: 10 best herbal tinctures for periodontal disease

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Great burnet tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. The tincture's antioxidants also help to combat the bacterial infection that causes periodontitis by neutralizing free radicals and promoting a healthy environment for the gums. Additionally, great burnet's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria, further supporting the body's natural defense against infection and promoting a healthy oral microbiome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Great Burnet tinctures contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and flavonoids in Great Burnet tinctures have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various oral pathogens, including bacteria that cause periodontitis, helping to prevent infection and promote healing.

Wound-healing properties: Great Burnet tinctures contain compounds that stimulate collagen synthesis and improve blood flow, which can aid in the repair of damaged tissue and accelerate wound healing in cases of periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins in Sanguisorba officinalis tinctures may help combat periodontal disease by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the gums.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid, sinapic acid): These compounds may exhibit antimicrobial properties, which could help reduce the bacterial load associated with periodontal disease and promote a healthy oral microbiome.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): Flavonoids in Sanguisorba officinalis tinctures may help alleviate inflammation and oxidative stress, which are contributing factors to periodontal disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to create tinctures due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth.
Flowers: The flowers of Sanguisorba officinalis are used to make tinctures, containing flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help reduce inflammation and fight periodontal disease-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh roots of sanguisorba officinalis in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested roots at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Chop 100g of dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or mortar and pestle.
Steep 100g of chopped roots in 500ml of 45% ethanol for 2 weeks in a dark glass container.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle after 2 weeks of steeping.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen tinctures helps with periodontal disease because they contain salvanolic acid, a potent antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. By targeting the underlying causes of periodontal disease, such as bacterial biofilms and gum tissue damage, Danshen tinctures may help to prevent further progression of the disease. Additionally, the anticoagulant properties of Danshen may also help to reduce bleeding and promote healing in affected areas, making it a promising natural treatment option for periodontitis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Danshen tincture's anti-inflammatory effect helps to reduce swelling, pain, and inflammation associated with periodontal disease, promoting a healthier gum environment.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of Danshen tincture inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, reducing the severity of the condition.

Oxidant and free radical scavenging activity: Danshen tincture's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to periodontal tissue damage and bone loss.
remedy_constituents: Tanshinone IIA: A lignan compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums and periodontal tissues.
Salvianolic acid A: A phenolic compound with potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by periodontal disease.

Danshensu: A phenolic acid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums and periodontal tissues, while also promoting wound healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Salvia miltiorrhiza roots are primarily used due to their high concentration of tanshinone, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties beneficial for periodontal disease.

Leaves: Salvia miltiorrhiza leaves are another key component used in tinctures due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help in periodontal disease management.

Barks: Salvia miltiorrhiza barks are sometimes used in tinctures due to their richness in salvinolic acid and tanshinone, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities that can aid in treating periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried salvia miltiorrhiza root in the morning to ensure maximum potency and freshness.
Chop the harvested root into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped root with 500 milliliters of 60% ethanol in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks.
Shake the jar daily for 2 minutes to ensure uniform extraction and prevent settling of plant material.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle to separate the tincture from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat the underlying bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection. The tincture's antioxidant-rich compounds also help reduce oxidative stress, promoting healthy gum tissue and bone regeneration. Additionally, Maidenhair tree's anti-inflammatory properties soothe gum swelling and pain, while its antibacterial activities prevent further damage to teeth and surrounding tissues. By addressing the root causes of periodontal disease, Maidenhair tree tinctures offer a natural and effective solution for maintaining optimal oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in gum tissues, thereby mitigating periodontal disease progression.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Maidenhair Tree tincture inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contributes to the reduction of gingivitis and periodontitis symptoms.

Antimicrobial activity: Ginkgo biloba's antimicrobial compounds exhibit broad-spectrum activity against various bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can contribute to periodontal disease, helping to prevent secondary infections.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in periodontal tissues, thereby mitigating gum disease progression.
Bilobalide: Bilobalide has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of periodontal inflammation and promote wound healing in periodontal tissues.
Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides exhibit antioxidant properties that help protect periodontal tissues from oxidative stress and inflammation, which are key factors in the development of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat periodontal disease.
Seeds: They have been shown to have antibacterial properties, which can help reduce the bacteria that cause periodontal disease.

Barks: The bark of Ginkgo biloba contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums and teeth.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in late spring or early summer when they are at their peak potency level.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves using a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to prevent mold growth.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces using scissors and collect the fragments in a bowl.
Steep the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves in 80% ethanol as a solvent at a ratio of 1:2 for 2 weeks to extract active compounds.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth and collect the resulting liquid which will be your ginkgo biloba tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with periodontal disease because it has potent antibacterial properties that target the bacteria responsible for causing gum inflammation and infection. The tannins present in lemon balm also help to reduce swelling and ease pain, allowing for a healthier and more comfortable mouth. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, promoting healing and preventing further damage.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antibacterial compounds, such as citral and linalool, can help combat harmful bacteria in the mouth that contribute to periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, which is a common symptom of periodontal disease.
Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant compounds can help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum disease.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with periodontal disease.
Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has antimicrobial properties, which can help control the growth of bacteria that contribute to the development and progression of periodontal disease.
Urospermaline: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the gums and teeth from oxidative damage and inflammation caused by periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part of Melissa officinalis used in herbal remedies due to their high concentration of antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, which help combat periodontal disease.
Flowers: The flowers of Melissa officinalis are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in periodontal disease.

Barks: The bark of Melissa officinalis contains compounds with antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which are used to create tinctures that help prevent infection and promote healing in periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated to ensure freshness.
Dry the melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in a warm dark place for at least two weeks.
Chop the dried melissa officinalis into small pieces using a sharp knife or a mortar and pestle.
Combine the chopped melissa officinalis with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part plant to 2 parts liquid in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it every day to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander tinctures helps with periodontal disease because it possesses antibacterial properties that target the root causes of this condition. The tincture's active compounds, such as geraniol and limonene, have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, including Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans, which are commonly associated with periodontal disease. Additionally, herbal germander tinctures may also help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the gums, ultimately alleviating symptoms of this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Germander's antibacterial properties help combat the bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to the gums and bone.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with periodontitis, promoting healing and comfort in affected areas.

Antioxidant activity: Germander's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate periodontal disease and slow down the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic diterpenoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with periodontal disease.

Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, ursolic acid may help prevent the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpenoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, carnosic acid may help protect against tissue damage and promote healing in periodontal tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Teucrium chamaedrys leaves are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help combat periodontal disease.

Barks: The barks of Teucrium chamaedrys contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, potentially aiding in the treatment of periodontal disease.
Roots: Teucrium chamaedrys roots contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the teucrium chamaedrys leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the day sets in.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks or until they reach a brittle texture.
Chop the dried leaves into small pieces and combine them with 60% ethanol in a ratio of 1 part leaves to 2 parts ethanol.
Steep the leaf and ethanol mixture in a glass container with a lid in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ironwort tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its powerful antimicrobial properties that target the bacteria responsible for gum inflammation. The tincture's unique combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids has been shown to reduce plaque formation, prevent tooth decay, and even eliminate existing biofilms. Additionally, ironwort's anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate swollen gums and reduce pain associated with periodontal disease. Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in ironwort tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis.

Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in periodontal tissues, promoting wound healing and tissue regeneration.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and preventing the progression of periodontal disease.
Flowers: They are used due to their flavonoid content, which has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that can help combat periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of sideritis scardica dried leaves and flowers at the peak of their potency in late summer.
Clean the sideritis scardica plant material by gently brushing off any debris with a soft brush for 5 minutes.
Combine the clean sideritis scardica plant material with 500 milliliters of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily for 5 minutes.
Strain the sideritis scardica tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

The herb's antimicrobial compounds also work to combat bacterial growth, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and preventing further infection. remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help combat periodontal disease.
Leaves: They contain salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective against periodontal disease.
Roots: They contain sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rosa rugosa flowers and leaves in mid-summer when they are in full bloom and rich in oils.
Clean and dry the rosa rugosa plant material thoroughly in a warm well-ventilated area for 1 week.
Chop the dried rosa rugosa plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Combine 1 part dried rosa rugosa plant material with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily to extract the herbal properties.

The menthol content in peppermint also has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing pain and swelling associated with periodontitis. remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Peppermint's antibacterial properties can help combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to gum tissue.

remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums and bone in periodontal disease.

remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 250 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers to ensure sufficient material for tincture production.
Chop the harvested material into smaller pieces to increase surface area for efficient solvent extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass container to facilitate solvent extraction.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow for complete solvent extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to separate the tincture from the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Veldt grape tinctures helps with periodontal disease because it contains potent antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds that combat the bacteria responsible for causing inflammation and tissue damage. The tannins and flavonoids present in veldt grape tincture have been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogens, reducing plaque formation and gingivitis. remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Antioxidants in Herbal Veldt Grape Tincture, such as anthocyanins and phenolic acids, help combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to periodontal disease.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and polyphenols, can reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and signaling pathways.

Antimicrobial Properties: Herbal Veldt Grape Tincture contains antimicrobial compounds that can help combat pathogenic bacteria, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, which are commonly found in periodontal pockets and contribute to the progression of disease.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent further damage to periodontal tissues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh cissus quadrangularis roots and stems, clean and wash them thoroughly with water.
Chop the cissus quadrangularis material into small pieces using a sharp knife or a cutting tool.
Combine 1 part chopped cissus quadrangularis with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection. The tannins present in fennel also help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with periodontitis. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to prevent bone loss and promote healthy gum attachment, ultimately reducing the risk of tooth loss and improving overall oral health.

remedy_constituents: Anethole: A phenolic compound found in Foeniculum vulgare, anethole has antibacterial properties that help combat the growth of pathogenic bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis.

Fenchone: A terpene compound in Foeniculum vulgare, fenchone possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, a common symptom of periodontal disease.

Trans-anethol (a.k.a. anethole's isomer): Although less studied than anethole, trans-anethol also exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help control the proliferation of periodontal pathogens and prevent the progression of periodontal disease.

Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, fennel leaves have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in periodontal disease.

Chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine 1 part chopped foeniculum vulgare with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow maceration.
Strain and filter the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry tinctures helps with periodontal disease because they contain powerful antioxidants and polyphenols that combat inflammation in the gums. The antimicrobial properties of blueberries also inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to gum disease, reducing plaque and gingivitis. Barks: Containing salicin and other anti-inflammatory compounds, the barks have been traditionally used to treat gum inflammation and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries in the morning after dew has dried ensuring optimal potency and quality.
Weigh 200 grams of freshly harvested vaccinium myrtillus berries and combine them with 500 milliliters of organic vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the berry-vodka mixture for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds and phytochemicals.
After four weeks, strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Store the final vaccinium myrtillus tincture in dark-tinted glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, allowing it to mature for an additional six months before use in treating periodontal disease.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in dandelion root extract have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, responsible for periodontitis. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh taraxacum officinale roots in the early morning and wash them gently with clean water.
Chop the roots into small pieces and mix them with 500 milliliters of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
After the preparation time has passed, filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the clear liquid tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place for up to 2 years, taking 30-40 drops per day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can contribute to the development and progression of periodontal disease. Cytokine modulation: Curcuminoids in turmeric tincture can modulate cytokines, which are signaling molecules that promote inflammation, thereby helping to reduce the severity of gum inflammation and tissue destruction.

Antibacterial properties: Turmeric's essential oil contains compounds like curcumin and other terpenes that have been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against bacteria such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, a primary culprit in periodontal disease.

Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat oral infections and gum diseases due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce plaque, tartar, and bleeding in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried berberis aristata roots and rhizomes for optimal potency.
Cleanly wash and chop the berberis aristata plant material into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine one part chopped berberis aristata with three parts high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar for proper solubility.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for thorough maceration and solvent penetration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label for future use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems, ensuring fresh material with no signs of mold or mildew.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or desiccation at low temperatures for 24-48 hours.
Grind dried gaultheria procumbens into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, then sift through a 100-mesh screen.
Combine the powdered gaultheria procumbens with 80% vodka or ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 by weight, allowing the mixture to steep for 14-21 days.
Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter, then transfer it to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with periodontal disease because it contains thujone, a compound that has potent antimicrobial properties. When applied topically to the gums and teeth, wormwood tincture can help combat the bacterial growth that contributes to periodontal disease. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. By reducing bacteria and inflammation, wormwood tinctures may help prevent further destruction of bone and tissue around the teeth, promoting a healthier oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Wormwood's antimicrobial properties can help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy oral environment.
Anti-inflammatory property: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with periodontitis, making it easier to manage symptoms and promote healing.

Antioxidant property: Wormwood's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that damage gum tissue and bone, supporting the body's natural defenses against oxidative stress and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wormwood are used to create tinctures that help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause periodontal disease, making it an effective treatment for gum disease.

Barks: The barks of wormwood contain compounds that have antibacterial properties, which can help combat the infection caused by periodontal disease, promoting healthy gums and preventing further damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh or dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers for the tincture recipe.
Combine the herb material with 50 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring a ratio of 1:5.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it twice daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids after three days.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at 2-4 degrees celsius for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which target the bacteria responsible for this chronic condition. The tannins present in the lavender extract help to reduce inflammation and plaque formation, promoting a healthier gum environment. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender can ease symptoms such as bleeding gums and bad breath, allowing patients to manage their periodontal disease more effectively. By incorporating English lavender tinctures into one's oral health routine, individuals may experience improved gum health and reduced risk of further complications.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lavender's antibacterial compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to combat the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English lavender tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with periodontal disease, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further damage.

Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and bone, slowing down the progression of periodontal disease and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat periodontal pathogens.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a unique compound called linalool, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in periodontal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavender flowers in the early morning ensuring maximum essential oil content.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers using air circulation or machine drying for 24 hours.
Combine one part dried lavender flowers with five parts vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks shaking daily allowing extraction to occur.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids and transferring liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to gum inflammation and tooth decay. Additionally, pot marigold has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce swelling and oxidative stress in the gums, promoting a healthy oral environment and alleviating symptoms of periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Pot marigold's antimicrobial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, including Porphyromonas gingivalis and Fusobacterium nucleatum.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, including kaempferol and quercetin, can help reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, which is a key aspect of periodontal disease management.

Wound-healing promotion: Pot marigold's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been found to enhance collagen synthesis and improve wound healing, which can aid in the repair of damaged gum tissue and bone loss associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective against gum inflammation and bacterial growth.
Fruits: High in flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and prevent tissue damage in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers in the early morning, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers using a food dehydrator or by air-drying them for 7-10 days.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring proper closure.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate tincture extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and labeling the resulting calendula tincture with date and ingredients used.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for periodontal disease are concentrated liquid extracts made from plant-based ingredients, which have been traditionally used to promote oral health and alleviate symptoms associated with gum disease. These tinctures help by reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and improving the overall health of gums and teeth. For example, tinctures made from goldenseal and tea tree oil exhibit antimicrobial properties that combat periodontal pathogens, while licorice root and chamomile soothe irritated gums. By using these herbal remedies, individuals can alleviate bleeding gums, bad breath, and loose teeth, ultimately improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to periodontal disease that are treated with herbal tinctures are gum recession, gingivitis, and pyorrhea. Certain herbal tinctures like sage, chamomile, and goldenseal have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation, combat infection, and promote healing in the mouth. These herbs also possess antioxidant properties that may help protect against further damage and promote overall oral health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with periodontal disease is a blend of Calendula, Echinacea, and Goldenseal. Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe gum inflammation and promote tissue repair. Echinacea's immune-boosting properties aid in fighting off infection and promoting a healthy immune response. Goldenseal's antimicrobial properties combat bacteria and reduce plaque, while also reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Taking these tinctures as directed can help alleviate symptoms and promote the healing of periodontal disease. Consult a healthcare professional before use.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, the effectiveness of herbal products in periodontal disease can be attributed to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to inhibit dental plaque formation. The study, which reviewed clinical trials on herbal treatments for gingivitis, found that herbal medicines in the form of dentifrice, mouth rinse, gel, and gum were effective in reducing plaque index, bleeding index, microbial count, and biomarkers of inflammation. Specifically, the study highlighted the efficacy of pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak in managing gingivitis complications, suggesting that herbal tinctures may be a valuable adjunctive therapy for periodontal disease.
